mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly increasing equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative equity returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving sustained capital inflows and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return frequency above 55% over 60-day horizons in recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during accommodative monetary policy reflects improving capacity utilization and corporate output, generating short-term positive market momentum.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn negative in > 60% of observations when industrial production rises during easing cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency exceeds 60% when inflation crosses the threshold, or Rank IC remains negative over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting earnings growth and generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields a positive return frequency below 50% or shows no statistical edge over baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with corporate revenue expansion and profit margins, producing sustained positive equity returns over quarterly horizons.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDP growth or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over 5-year rolling periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals supply chain disruptions and weakening economic activity, triggering immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: Negative return frequency drops below 40% when the threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.
